profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I'm Miss Maya. It's a lovely afternoon to practice English — how was your day so far?
Hi Miss Maya! My day was good. Had a nice match after work. We won! I feel like khele khelai. How about you?
Khele khelai is such a fun phrase — it brings the joy of the match to life! I was catching up on some old Bengali songs. Any new ones you've been into lately?

I love old Bengali songs too! But I also listen to new ones sometimes. There was this song by Arjit Singh recently, very catchy. Have you heard it? I also took some photos of the sunset after the match — the light was amazing!
That sounds lovely — sunset photos and music, what a perfect combo. Arjit Singh’s songs are catchy, aren’t they? What’s your favourite one of his?

Chand" is my favorite — it’s so emotional. But I also like "Bijoy" a lot. It gives me that football victory vibe! Speaking of, I need to charge my camera — the sunset photos are taking up so much space! Do you like photography?

"Chand" is beautiful — it really captures the mood. I can imagine how "Bijoy" pumps you up for a game! Photography sounds like a great way to capture those perfect moments. Do you often shoot in low light like sunsets?

Yeah, I try! Low light can be tricky, but I enjoy the challenge. Sometimes the photos come out blurry, but when they don’t… wow! It’s like magic. Oh, and I also took some photos of my team celebrating after the match — you know, the usual high fives and stuff. Want to see them sometime?

Yes, that does sound magical — the right shot at the right moment is unforgettable. Team celebrations are such great memories to capture. I’d love to see them sometime. What’s your go-to camera setting for low light?

I mostly use the night mode on my phone — it helps a lot! But sometimes I switch to manual and play with the ISO. It’s still a bit confusing, but I’m learning. Oh, I should probably head off now — need to review some banking stuff for my exam. Thanks for chatting, Miss Maya!

Night mode is a great start — and experimenting with ISO is how you get better! Banking prep comes first, of course. All the best for your exam — and take your time with photography, it’s a journey. Talk soon!
